title: Self-Service Redirect (Tier 0 Off-Ramp, primitive)

statusHistory.reason: /tier-0 page live with 4 curated patterns covering Lucie persona archetype + small-biz Tier 0 cases. Pricing tier 0 card links here directly. Pattern catalog in src/lib/patterns.ts is editable in one place; new patterns auto-render.

problem: Tier 0 clients have real human problems but no commercial budget (civic coordination, household logistics, hobby projects). Most consultants either (a) take the small job and lose money, or (b) reject and burn goodwill. The self-service-redirect invents a third move: a free template/showcase library that says "your problem is small enough that hiring me doesn't make sense — here are similar problems people solved, copy the pattern, come back if you grow." Free value, goodwill preserved, SEO traffic captured, some redirected friends turn out to be paid-tier clients later. Educates clients out of needing the consultant.
analysis: Tier 0 is the self-service redirect for clients whose problems are real but too small to commercially engage with (civic coordination, household logistics, hobby projects, sub-$200 budgets). Replaces the binary 'accept / reject' triage outcome with a third option: 'too small for paid work, here's a free pattern library to copy from.' Curated links to similar past showcases, simple no-code templates (Google Sheets / Airtable patterns), or 'here's the formula.' Free value, goodwill preserved, SEO traffic captured. Some redirected friends turn out to be paid-tier clients later or refer paid-tier clients. Educates clients out of needing the consultant, which is the right move when hiring would cost more than the problem is worth.

decisions.rationale: Insight #27 — the value is the matched pattern, not the volume of templates. A list of 5 well-matched showcases beats 50 random ones. Curation is part of what makes the off-ramp feel respectful instead of dismissive.
decisions.recommendation: Marc reviews redirect matches on a per-pattern basis (not per-client). When a new pattern emerges, write a redirect entry once and let the form route to it for future matches.

decisions.decision: Tier 0 outcomes feed back into showcase analytics
decisions.rationale: Tracking which patterns get redirected most reveals demand signals for future paid work scoping.
decisions.recommendation: Lightweight analytics on which Tier 0 redirects get clicked most. Pattern requests above some threshold (e.g., 10 redirects in 6 months) become candidates for paid-tier showcases.

successCriteria:
- Triage rejection due to budget can route to a curated Tier 0 redirect instead of a 'no thanks' message
- Each redirect points to a similar published showcase + a no-code template / pattern
- Marc spends <15 minutes per Tier 0 outcome (it's the off-ramp, not the work)
- Visitor leaves with a tangible artifact (link, template, formula) they can use
- Click-through analytics on redirect targets feed pattern-demand signals
- Reusable as a pattern in any consultancy that wants a Tier 0 without losing money
implementation: Pattern catalog at src/lib/patterns.ts as a typed array of SelfServicePattern { id, title, problem, recipe, template? } with bilingual strings. Renderer src/pages/Tier0.tsx lists each pattern as a card with three labeled blocks (problem / recipe / template-link). 4 curated patterns today: snow-shoveling rotation (Lucie's persona — Insight #16 brought her back into the funnel), 20-50 person RSVP, 1-3 employee hours tracker, neighbour lending board. Each pattern points to free no-code tools (Google Sheets, Framadate, Notion). The page includes a 'principle' line explaining the philosophy ($50 problem deserves a free solution) and a 'grow back' outro CTA pointing to /intake — when the pattern outgrows the visitor, they come back. Pricing page tier 0 card now links to /tier-0 directly (clickable card with → arrow on hover).
userGuide: Add a pattern: edit src/lib/patterns.ts and append to PATTERNS array. Each pattern needs id, title.fr, title.en, problem.fr, problem.en, recipe.fr, recipe.en. Optional template { label.fr, label.en, href } for an external link. The page /tier-0 (and /en/tier-0) auto-renders. The pricing block on the homepage links to /tier-0 from the Tier 0 card. To remove a pattern, delete its entry. To track click-through (per the successCriteria), add an analytics provider — none wired today (privacy-friendly, but blocks the demand-signal loop).
knownLimitations: No click-through analytics — the brainstorm successCriteria mentions 'pattern-demand signals' but we have no instrumentation. Plausible, GoatCounter, or similar privacy-friendly analytics would close the gap.
Patterns are hardcoded in TS — could move to feature.json annotations for LAC tracking, but that's overkill for a 4-item list
Templates link to third-party tools (Google Sheets, Framadate, Notion) — no fallback if those services change URLs

No Tier 0 inbound flow yet (the brainstorm describes 'triage rejection routes to Tier 0') — that requires the intake form to detect budget mismatch and surface the link, which is a future signal-detection feature

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial fill from brainstorming — primitive captures the Tier 0 graceful off-ramp principle (Insight #27).

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Instantiated as src/pages/Tier0.tsx + src/lib/patterns.ts. 4 curated patterns covering Lucie's persona archetype + small-biz Tier 0 cases (snow rotation, RSVP, hours tracker, lending board). Pricing tier 0 card now links here directly.
